brief_justification: Paper A is a solid-state gravitational-wave detector whose core machinery is signal detection via cryogenic single-phonon counting statistics, resonant long-integration versus broad-band coincidence-tagged searches; Paper B's machinery is a quantum-circuit algorithm that applies controlled unitary transformations to ancilla qubits to map entanglement invariants (concurrence, 3-tangle) onto output measurement probabilities. Beyond the generic notion of extracting a quantity from measurement outcomes, there is no shared governing process, equation, or role-for-role structure, so the machinery differs.
